text.title: laravel-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for laravel-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: N+1: one SELECT per user to load organization. Use User::with('organization')->get() instead.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Mass assignment with all() sends every request field to the model, including non-fillable fields an attacker may inject. Always use $request->validated().

failure-mode.description: Mail::to($user)->send(new WelcomeMail($user)) inside a DB::transaction block.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Mail sends before the transaction commits. If the transaction rolls back, the user record does not exist but the email is already delivered. Use deliver_later after the transaction.

failure-mode.description: Resolving services via app(Service::class) instead of constructor injection.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: app() bypasses the container's autowiring and breaks testability — the test cannot substitute a mock via the constructor.
